questions.text: The longest chord of a circle is called:
questions.explanation: Diameter is the longest chord of a circle. It passes through the center and is twice the radius.

questions.text: Equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the center.
questions.explanation: Equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the center. This is a fundamental property of circles.

questions.text: What is a tangent to a circle?
questions.correctAnswer: A line that touches the circle at exactly one point.
questions.explanation: A tangent is a line that intersects the circle at exactly one point. The radius at the point of contact is perpendicular to the tangent.

questions.text: The angle in a semicircle is ________ degrees.
questions.explanation: The angle subtended by a diameter at any point on the circle is 90° (angle in a semicircle is a right angle).
